feedback.text: Dear My opinion is similar to most of the people who replied to this post, but I would like to explain my point again and help with my video games, without being stolen: I think it is important to be able to keep games I spent on my own money. I am still quite spared, but if gamblers can just remove my games at any time, there is little help to buy them, if we already spend so much money on them, I personally think that we are seen as a minimum as customers, because if you buy something in the shop, they cannot take it back without you really having a choice, and even then you will still (partially) get your money back. I personally think that they are products, and also long-term products, not just a hamburger who eat you and no longer has it, but the same as a washing machine, something that works in the long term. My point actually comes at: people buying games are perceived as consumers/sub-standard, whereas it would be more logical to see us as customers, because we do the same, but just for non-physical products. It is rather important that we still keep something of our money we spend – another way to look at this is: suggest that hanks the world, except 5 businesses, have every house, every car, everything you can have. The only way to ‘have’ something is renting from these companies, but they have added rules everywhere that allow them to steal your things you rent and no longer have to reduce them. So you rent a house for 3 years, and a car for 2 years, the first year goes well, you can use your car, and live in your house, but once you get out of work, your car is away, this is a long walk to your home, you see you don’t have access, now you can replace the houses and the car with video games, and that is what really happened, and what video publishers really want to do. However, I do not think that I would like to live in a society I would like to live in. But here we are going silent, as we still have the chance to stop it, I think we should take this opportunity, of course at your choice, let you do the giga companies what they want to do, not stop them at all, and everyone lowers their online rights until we just pay but have nothing, or choose to make sure that we can still play our games. this does not mean that the video games are still connected to the internet (otherwise they will see it), but if the servers are down, we can still play what we are holding without always being afraid that we will lose everything to take your time.
